item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's what bothers me most. If it were at least a really good entry, that would make up for it enough that I could forgive it. But it's…mediocre.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A good bit of shortform weirdness, but one which totally fails to grab me for whatever reason. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I miss seeing skips like this; makes me hopeful that implication, elision, and just straight-up "leaving the reader hungry for more" aren't a dying art. Easiest +1 I gave this week.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That would be SCP-4739, Alastair Grey, which follows Antimemetics Division members around and eats their memories. This is SCP-4987, the monster which… uh, follows Antimemetics Division chief Marion Wheeler around and eats her memories.

Okay, never mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wouldn't that contradiction naturally happen when no one can remember the thing and there is no file on it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Novote for now. It needs more, as everyone else is saying. Otherwise, I quite enjoy it, although it does contradict this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No vote. Not to parrot what everyone else is saying, but while there's not anything done badly here, there's not a lot of substance. It's just a somewhat interesting idea that isn't expanded upon. Plus you have to wonder how the Foundation duscovered this in the first place.

If this gets expanded on, it'll probably be an upvote for me, but for now I'm neutral.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote, and shame it’s not Alistair Grey

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And the award for best article derived from R.E.M. lyrics goes to…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Masterful as always. I actually put 'Stand' on while reading this.

+1, facing north.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked the containment procedures, really set you up for some interesting mystery involving a familiar scientific phenomenon. Unfortunately as mentioned by others, it just doesn't do enough to develop it. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My headcanon is that in order to cull populations from facing north, we just label R.E.M. as dadrock and move on with our lives.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tentative upvote because I enjoy the concept, but I really wish there was more here.

As a reader, I'm left with a ton of questions and no answers. How did the Foundation discover that neural activity anomalously affects magnetic north? Do neurons only affect magnetic north, or is the magnetic south affected by them equally opposite as well? What effects would an immediate reversal of Earth's magnetic poles have? What does "Operation Mindful Sustainability" entail? Obviously such a document exists in-universe, so why don't we get to see even a heavily redacted version of it?

While writing this, I'm finding myself growing more and more frustrated with the sheer lack of substance here. Enough so that I may just reverse my +1 to a -1, at least unless this thing gets a serious content drop.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Novote. There’s nothing bad about this article but there also isn’t anything that strikes me as particularly interesting.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Stand in the place where you live, now face north.

Think about destruction, wonder where your mind went right now.

Original picture is by Cavit, released under CC-BY 4.0 and modified by me.
